_note: GOLD fixtures for sponsor-match ranking eval (Phase 0). Copy this to sponsor-fixtures.json and FILL IN. For each prompt: paste the real sponsor description, then hand-rank the scholars you believe should surface, each with a relevance GRADE. The harness scores the ranker's actual output against this (nDCG + rank-correlation). Nothing in Phase 1/2/3 is 'verified' without a filled fixture.
_grades: 3 = world-class / the person you'd name first. 2 = strong, genuinely on-topic. 1 = marginal / tangential but not wrong. 0 = FALSE POSITIVE — matches lexically but is the wrong domain (the failure class the ranker must push down). List 0s too; they anchor the bottom.
_rarity_case: SUPERSEDED — DO NOT ENCODE A RARITY PREFERENCE (#1698). The Phase-2/3 design §14 asked for a probe where a scholar expert in a RARE area out-ranks an expert in a COMMON one, graded rare=3 / common=2. The ranker deliberately does not do that, and grading it that way scores the system down for behaving as designed. Corpus rarity anti-correlates with topical centrality (it ranked a disease's own mechanisms above the disease), so it was removed from the fusion weight entirely — see sponsor-match-contract.ts (`weightFactor`). GRADE ON TOPICALITY ALONE: who actually works on what the funder is asking for. The rare-disease prompts (scleroderma, cystic fibrosis, ALS) are KEPT, but as thin-corpus COVERAGE probes — can the ranker still find the right people when a topic has few papers — not as rarity-preference probes.
_how_to_run: See sponsor-README.md. Score a captured ranking: ACTUAL=run.json ./sponsor-eval.sh sponsor-fixtures.json  (run.json = {"<prompt id>": ["cwid", ...ranked...]}). Self-check the math: ./sponsor-eval.sh --selftest.

prompts.paste: <<< REPLACE: paste the real sponsor description for the cancer-metabolism prompt >>>
prompts.notes: Discriminator is oncology CONTEXT — reward metabolism ∧ cancer. A world leader in cancer lipid metabolism with one indexed paper should top a microbiome/T2D/cardiometabolic researcher with several 'metabolism' papers (concept-ranking spec §1a/§1b).

ideal.note: why this person is a perfect match
false positive — e.g. microbiome 'metabolism', wrong domain

prompts.paste: <<< REPLACE: paste the real sponsor description for the immuno-oncology prompt >>>
prompts.notes: Mechanistic domain terms (T-cell exhaustion, checkpoint, tumor microenvironment) should bridge to people whose work IS immuno-onc even when their abstracts don't echo the sponsor's surface vocabulary.
